title: Bank of America Turns Bullish on 2 Big Homebuilding Names
text: Bank of America Securities gave two major homebuilding stocks double upgrades to "buy" from "underperform" this morning; Toll Brothers Inc (NYSE:TOL) and PulteGroup, Inc. (NYSE:PHM).The analyst noted a favorable risk/reward profile for these stocks, as the sector has underperformed so far this year.

title: These 3 Homebuilders Fit the Value Stock Profile
text: Each one of these three residential construction stocks fit the traditional value stock profile: low price-earnings ratios, not too far from book value, decent earnings records, low debt profiles and paying dividends. These are the sorts of valuation metrics Benjamin Graham wrote about in his classic works on the subject.

title: PulteGroup's Built to Honor® Program Breaks Ground on Two Mortgage-Free Homes for Deserving Army Veterans and their Families in Houston
text: HOUSTON--(BUSINESS WIRE)--U.S. Army Sergeant James Ford and Army Specialist Kisha Dorsey have broken ground on their brand-new homes, donated by national homebuilder PulteGroup. text: The homebuilder and mortgage insurance stocks today sell at P/E ratios that are fractions of the S&P 500. Realtors retain high P/E ratios even after significant sell-offs. title: PulteGroup (PHM) Beats Q4 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
text: PulteGroup (PHM) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of 9.61% and 4.68%, respectively, for the quarter ended December 2021. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: PulteGroup profit and revenue rise above expectations, as average home sales price jumps 10%
text: PulteGroup Inc. PHM, +2.81% reported Tuesday fourth-quarter profit and revenue that rose above expectations, as a slight decline in net new orders was offset by a big jump in order values. The home builder's stock was still inactive in the premarket.

title: PulteGroup Reports Fourth Quarter 2021 Financial Results
text: ATLANTA--(BUSINESS WIRE)--PulteGroup, Inc. (NYSE: PHM) announced today financial results for its fourth quarter ended December 31, 2021. For the quarter, the Company reported net income of $663 million, or $2.61 per share. Adjusted net income for the period was $637 million, or $2.51 per share, after excluding a $23 million net pre-tax benefit from adjustments to insurance-related reserves and a tax benefit of $9 million from deferred tax valuation allowance adjustments recorded in the period.
